Tests string comparison with null characters in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the lexicographical order of strings containing null characters. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the string `"x\0a"` is less than `"x\0b"`, and `"x\0b"` is less than `"x\0c"`. The test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles string comparisons when null characters (`\0`) are present within the strings. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on string comparison behavior in JavaScript.
